results.description: NOT IN with subquery — poor performance vs NOT EXISTS or anti-join
results.original_sql: SELECT P_PARTKEY, P_NAME, P_RETAILPRICE FROM PART WHERE P_PARTKEY NOT IN ( SELECT L_PARTKEY FROM LINEITEM WHERE L_RETURNFLAG = 'R' )
results.optimized_sql: SELECT P_PARTKEY, P_NAME, P_RETAILPRICE FROM PART WHERE P_PARTKEY NOT IN (SELECT L_PARTKEY FROM LINEITEM WHERE L_RETURNFLAG = 'R')

results.description: DISTINCT on large result set instead of GROUP BY — implicit cross join syntax
results.original_sql: SELECT DISTINCT C_NAME, C_NATIONKEY, O_ORDERSTATUS FROM CUSTOMER, ORDERS WHERE CUSTOMER.C_CUSTKEY = ORDERS.O_CUSTKEY
results.optimized_sql: SELECT C_NAME, C_NATIONKEY, O_ORDERSTATUS FROM CUSTOMER JOIN ORDERS ON CUSTOMER.C_CUSTKEY = ORDERS.O_CUSTKEY

verified_changes.action: Removed DISTINCT from the SELECT clause
verified_changes.reason: The DISTINCT keyword is redundant because the join operation inherently removes duplicates.

verified_changes.action: Rewrote the join using explicit JOIN syntax
verified_changes.reason: Using explicit JOIN syntax improves readability and allows for more control over join conditions.

results.description: Function on filter column prevents partition pruning — YEAR/MONTH wrapping date column
results.original_sql: SELECT L_ORDERKEY, L_QUANTITY, L_EXTENDEDPRICE FROM LINEITEM WHERE YEAR(L_SHIPDATE) = 1995 AND MONTH(L_SHIPDATE) = 3
results.optimized_sql: SELECT L_ORDERKEY, L_QUANTITY, L_EXTENDEDPRICE FROM LINEITEM WHERE L_SHIPDATE BETWEEN '1995-03-01' AND '1995-03-31'

verified_changes.action: Replaced YEAR(L_SHIPDATE) = 1995 AND MONTH(L_SHIPDATE) = 3 with L_SHIPDATE BETWEEN '1995-03-01' AND '1995-03-31'
verified_changes.reason: This change makes the predicate sargable, allowing the use of an index if available.

verified_changes.action: Applied a sargable predicate to reduce the number of rows scanned.
verified_changes.reason: Reducing the number of rows scanned can improve performance by minimizing the amount of data read from disk.
